```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
p0    <- sample(c(0.3, 0.4), 1)
p1    <- sample(c(0.6, 0.7), 1)
n     <- sample(c(10, 15), 1)
alpha <- 0.05

## Ponto critico: menor k tal que P(X >= k | p0) <= alpha
## Equivalente: k = qbinom(1-alpha, n, p0) quando P(X > qbinom) < alpha
k_val <- qbinom(1 - alpha, n, p0)
## Verificar: P(X >= k_val | p0)
## se P(X >= k_val) > alpha, incrementar k_val
while (1 - pbinom(k_val - 1, n, p0) > alpha) {
  k_val <- k_val + 1
}

## Poder: P(X >= k_val | p1)
correct <- round(1 - pbinom(k_val - 1, n, p1), 4)
w1 <- round(pbinom(k_val - 1, n, p1), 4)
w2 <- round(1 - pbinom(k_val, n, p1), 4)
w3 <- round(1 - pbinom(k_val - 1, n, p0), 4)
w4 <- round(1 - pbinom(k_val - 1, n, p1) - 0.1, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X \sim \text{Bin}(n,p)$ com $n = `r n`$, testando $H_0:p = `r p0`$ vs. $H_1:p = `r p1`$ com $\alpha = 0{,}05$. O teste de NP rejeita $H_0$ quando $X \geq k$, com $k = `r k_val`$ tal que $P(X \geq `r k_val` \mid p = `r p0`) \leq 0{,}05$. O poder do teste e $P(X \geq `r k_val` \mid p = `r p1`)$:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Poder $= P(X \geq `r k_val` \mid p = `r p1`) = 1 - P(X \leq `r k_val-1` \mid p = `r p1`) = 1 - \text{pbinom}(`r k_val-1`, `r n`, `r p1`) = `r correct`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S2Q10_NP_binomial_poder
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
